channel.title: The Ant Fund
channel.description: Slow, steady wealth for everyday investors — calculators, education, and clear thinking on the road to financial independence.

item.title: Your FIRE number: every major method, calculated and compared
item.description: There's one equation underneath every FIRE calculator. The whole debate is about how to set one number in it. A field guide to seven methods — Bengen's 4% rule, Morningstar's forward-looking SWR, CAPE-linked rates, Guyton-Klinger guardrails, dynamic withdrawals, and the variants (Lean, Fat, Coast, Barista) — plus the architecture that combines them.

item.title: Will my money last? A beginner's guide to the Monte Carlo retirement calculator
item.description: What a Monte Carlo retirement calculator actually is, how it works under the hood, and how to read every number it gives you — explained in plain English.

item.title: Calls and puts, explained: a beginner's guide to buying and selling options
item.description: What calls and puts actually are, who's on each side of the trade, where the money flows, and the four positions that make up every options strategy — written for someone who has never traded an option before.

item.title: The Wheel: a foundations guide to selling options for income
item.description: What calls and puts actually are, how the Wheel strategy generates income, and where premium selling really earns — a careful look at the math behind options income.

item.title: Hello, world — a pipeline test post
item.description: A throwaway post used during initial setup to verify the blog pipeline end-to-end. Delete this once a real article ships.

item.title: The 4% rule, explained for normal people
item.description: It's a starting point, not a law of physics. Where the 4% rule came from, what it actually claims, and why it still holds up after forty years.

item.title: Index funds vs. picking stocks: what the math actually says
item.description: The case for indexing isn't about cynicism — it's about humility, fees, and what twenty years of data tell us about beating the market.
